body_txt: When Four-Fingered Hands aren't simple enough, robots can take it a step further with only two . Like the claws of a crab and lobster. Whether crafted from a single piece or separated by a palm facsimile, the fingers form a U-like shape that pinches down to grasp. A common feature of a Tin-Can Robot . Besides robots, cyborgs or beings with an Artificial Limb can also feature one of these. One reason a writer might resort to this is because it's way easier to draw a character with fewer fingers, so a robot/mecha character can be drawn with just two fingers. And of course, if grabbing things is the only action a robot ever has to perform with its hands, then there's no sense in making the hand more complex than this. See also Power Pincers .
